calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defualt Judgment Against Defendant Charles Michael Yim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-14-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment against Defendant Charles Michael Yim.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:54:30 a.m. and concluding at 10:14:17 a.m.

Plaintiff Digital Capital, LLC, a Florida Limited Liability Company appearing by Paul Michael represented by Matthew Hess, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Nathanael McKervey appearing represented by Matthew Hess, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Chales Michael Yim not appearing.

PAUL MICHAEL and NATHANAEL MCKERVEY are sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Supplemental Declaration showing wire transfer of $75,000.00 filed Jun-12-2023. Proof of transfer shown in Exhibit K attached to declaration.

Court and counsel confer re: status of case, summary of case, and offer of proof.

Court examines NATHANAEL MCKERVEY.
Court examines PAUL MICHAEL.

The matter is submitted.
COURT MAKES THE FOLLOWING FINDINGS:

1. Complaint filed Nov-25-2000 is the operative pleading.
2. Judgment rendered would resolve the entire dispute.
3. Defendant was served the complaint on Jan-06-2021. Proof of Service filed Jan-07-2021.
4. Defendant Cointopia, Inc. and DOES 1-50 were dismissed on Jun-24-2021.
5. Default entered as to Defendant Charles Michael Yim on Feb-18-2021.

Based on the documents filed, evidence provided, the testimony of Plaintiff's witnesses, and statements made by Plaintiffs' counsel, the Court finds Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence establishing all causes of action in the complaint. Plaintiffs did wire funds to Defendant based on his false representations.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Digital Capital, LLC, a Florida Limited Liability Company, and against Defendant Charles Michael Yim, as follows:

Plaintiffs' request for pre-judgment interest is DENIED.

1. Damages: $290,000.00
2. Costs: $1,677.11

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $291,677.11

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Nathanael McKervey, and against Defendant Charles Michael Yim, as follows:

1. Damages: $65,000.00
2. Costs: $1,919.07

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $66,919.07

Proposed judgments signed.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2023 Continued To Jul-26-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defualt Judgment Against Defendant Charles Michael Yim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-12-2023 Continued To Jun-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment against Defendant Charles Michael Yim.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:08:58 a.m. and concluding at 10:28:33 a.m.

Plaintiff Digital Capital, LLC represented by Matthew Hess, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Charles Michael Yim appearing in pro per and placed under oath via Zoom Video Conference.

NATHANIEL MCKERVEY and PAUL MICHAEL are sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Defendant is present.
Default as to Defendant Charles Michael Yim on Feb-18-2021.
Order Denying Defendant Charles Michael Yim's Motion for Discretionary Relief to Set Aside Default filed Aug-17-2021.

Court determines that Defendant Charles Michael Yim is not permitted to participate in today's hearing based on the denial of its request to set aside default but he is free to observe the proceedings.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.
Plaintiff requests damages for Paul Michael in the sum of $290,000.00, plus pre-judgment interest and damages for Nathaniel McKervey in the sum of $65,000.00, plus pre-judgment interest.

Court examines NATHANIEL MCKERVEY.
Court examines PAUL MICHAEL.

Plaintiff is ordered to file and deliver courtesy copies of supplemental declaration including evidence of wires and dates it is seeking interest. Proposed judgment shall also be submitted.

Court orders matter continued to Jun-14-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for further hearing. Witnesses NATHANIEL MCKERVEY and PAUL MICHAEL are ordered to return.
